message: I grew up as the person who *forgot* to charge their phone before a road trip. Not exactly emergency-ready material. So when a big storm rolled through last year, I actually surprised myself—I'd filled some water jugs the night before, checked our flashlight batteries, and even made sure the family knew where to meet if we lost power. My mom called me during the storm to check on us, and I got to be the calm one for once. Didn't need to use any of it, thank goodness, but *being* ready? That felt good. Turns out you don't have to be naturally cautious to handle a storm well—you just need a little planning ahead. What's one small thing you've done lately that made you feel more ready for whatever weather comes your way?
